---
title: "More kroki formats"
---

Beyond [PlantUML](plantuml.qmd), eight formats render through the same
[kroki](https://kroki.io/) backend: D2, C4-PlantUML, Structurizr, erd,
ditaa, pikchr [@kernighan1984pic], svgbob, and TikZ. Each was verified
working end-to-end before being enabled — and several kroki formats were
deliberately excluded as broken or unsuitable (see
[Formats](../formats.qmd)). One example of each, below; click to zoom.

## D2

[D2](https://d2lang.com/) ([docs](https://d2lang.com/tour/intro)) is
Terrastruct's declarative diagram language — the most modern entry in
the boxes-and-arrows space, with clean syntax, first-class containers,
and multiple layout engines. It excels at software architecture and
system diagrams where you want good-looking output without thinking
about layout. @fig-d2 is authored inline in this page's source:

```{.d2 #fig-d2 fig-cap="The render pipeline, in D2"}
direction: right
filter -> cache: lookup
cache -> renderer: miss
renderer -> svg: emit
svg -> document: native figure
```

## C4-PlantUML

[C4-PlantUML](https://github.com/plantuml-stdlib/C4-PlantUML) layers the
[C4 model](https://c4model.com/)'s vocabulary — person, system,
container, component — over PlantUML as ready-made macros. It excels at
architecture documentation that stays visually consistent across teams,
because the notation enforces C4's abstraction levels instead of leaving
styling to each author.

![System context in C4](figures/context.c4){#fig-c4}

[view source: `context.c4`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/context.c4){.source-link}

## Structurizr

The [Structurizr DSL](https://docs.structurizr.com/dsl)
takes C4 further with a model-once, view-many philosophy: you describe
the *model* (people, systems, containers, relationships) and then
declare as many views of it as you need, each auto-laid-out. It excels
when an architecture is documented from multiple angles that must never
drift apart.

![The same idea in the Structurizr DSL](figures/workspace.structurizr){#fig-struct}

[view source: `workspace.structurizr`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/workspace.structurizr){.source-link}

## erd

[erd](https://github.com/BurntSushi/erd) is a minimal
entity-relationship notation: entities in brackets, one attribute per
line, cardinality in a single `1--*` line. It excels at quick relational
sketches during schema design — when DBML feels like more ceremony than
the idea deserves.

![Entity-relationship, tersely](figures/schema.erd){#fig-erd}

[view source: `schema.erd`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/schema.erd){.source-link}

## ditaa

[ditaa](https://ditaa.sourceforge.net/) converts ASCII-art boxes and
arrows into polished, rounded, color-tagged diagrams (`cGRE`, `cBLU`
mark fill colors). It excels when the source must stay legible as plain
text — README diagrams, code comments — while still rendering nicely in
published output.

![ASCII art in, polished boxes out](figures/flow.ditaa){#fig-ditaa}

[view source: `flow.ditaa`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/flow.ditaa){.source-link}

## pikchr

[pikchr](https://pikchr.org/) ([manual](https://pikchr.org/home/doc/trunk/doc/userman.md))
is D. Richard Hipp's modern descendant of Kernighan's PIC
[@kernighan1984pic], built for and used throughout the SQLite and Fossil
documentation. It excels at precise technical line diagrams where
relative placement ("arrow; box; arrow right") beats coordinates.

![PIC's modern descendant](figures/pipeline.pikchr){#fig-pikchr}

[view source: `pipeline.pikchr`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/pipeline.pikchr){.source-link}

## svgbob

[svgbob](https://github.com/ivanceras/svgbob) vectorizes ASCII drawings
while *preserving* their hand-typed aesthetic — where ditaa polishes,
svgbob stays faithful. It excels when the ASCII drawing itself is the
artifact you care about and you just want it crisp at any size.

![ASCII art, faithfully vectorized](figures/boxes.svgbob){#fig-svgbob}

[view source: `boxes.svgbob`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/boxes.svgbob){.source-link}

## TikZ

[TikZ](https://tikz.dev/) is LaTeX's native graphics system and the
gold standard for publication figures: mathematical precision,
typographic consistency with the surrounding document, and an enormous
package ecosystem. It excels at academic figures that must match the
paper's typography exactly. Sources must be complete
`\documentclass{standalone}` documents.

![A complete standalone LaTeX document](figures/nodes.tikz){#fig-tikz}

[view source: `nodes.tikz`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/nodes.tikz){.source-link}

---

Every kroki format also works as a file reference or inline block, with
the same captions, cross-references, and caching as local backends.
Network is needed only on cache misses; self-host with
`livefigures: kroki-url:` for private diagrams.
